hero_summary: Athar Kurd is a very rare maqam as a full independent maqam. It is understood as a blend between Nawa Athar / Nikriz and Kurd, and for that reason it is placed closer to the Nikriz sphere than to plain Kurd.
short_identity: A very rare maqam that begins with Jins Athar Kurd on the tonic and is followed by Jins Hijaz Kar on the ghammaz (the fifth degree), with its identity arising from the exact structural blend between Kurd and Nawa Athar / Nikriz.

quick_facts.karar: The tonic on which Jins Athar Kurd is built
quick_facts.lower_jins: Jins Athar Kurd on the tonic
quick_facts.upper_jins: Jins Hijaz Kar on the ghammaz (the fifth degree)
quick_facts.character: Very rare, structurally poised between Kurd and Nawa Athar / Nikriz, with a raised fourth as its most decisive sign
quick_facts.common_start: It begins by clearly establishing Jins Athar Kurd, then ascends to Hijaz Kar on the fifth

sayr.summary: Athar Kurd is very rare as a full independent maqam. Modern references describe it as one of the relatively late-used and peripheral maqamat within the broader Arabic network of ajnas and maqamat. It is usually linked to the Nawa Athar / Nikriz sphere more than to plain Kurd, because its structure is understood as a blend between Nawa Athar and Kurd, and that is the reason for its name: Athar + Kurd.
resting_tones: The tonic on which Jins Athar Kurd is built
The fifth degree: the ghammaz, because it carries Hijaz Kar in the direct practical explanation
The raised fourth: the central auditory marker that immediately separates the maqam from plain Kurd
motion_notes: The direct practical definition of the maqam is: Jins Athar Kurd on the tonic, followed by Jins Hijaz Kar on the ghammaz (the fifth degree)
Athar Kurd can be reached either from Jins Kurd by raising the fourth by a semitone, or from Jins Nikriz by lowering the second by a semitone
Its identity is not understood from scale alone, but from the way the compound relation between the two ajnas is made audible
A common mistake is to perform it as if it were simply Kurd with one altered note, and that causes the maqam’s identity to disappear
common_path: Establish Jins Athar Kurd as a self-standing entity
Bring out the raised fourth clearly
Ascend to Hijaz Kar on the fifth
Return while preserving the maqam’s structural relation to both Kurd and Nawa Athar / Nikriz together

common_modulations: Athar Kurd can be derived from Kurd by raising the fourth by a semitone, and this is the clearest and most direct structural distinction between the two
It can also be understood as a form close to Nawa Athar / Nikriz after lowering the second by a semitone, so it stands structurally between the two worlds rather than being a simple Kurd branch

It shares with Saba Zamzama a certain rarity and relatively modern context, but Athar Kurd remains more explicitly tied to Nikriz / Nawa Athar, while Saba Zamzama belongs to the Saba world with a lowered second
The most important point to state explicitly is that Athar Kurd is extremely rare as a full maqam, and appears more often as a fleeting color or modulation inside other maqamic contexts

examples.title: ya fagri nurak halli w ban
examples.note: Inside Arabic Music cites this as the only full, clearly recognized complete example they know for Athar Kurd as an independent maqam

reference_recordings.title: ya fagri nurak halli w ban
reference_recordings.note: The strongest modern documented example of Athar Kurd as a full independent maqam

ui_notes.hero_badge: Branch of the Nikriz / Nawa Athar family
